definition: A FlowMCP API template defining endpoints, parameters, and outputs in a standardized v4.0.0 format.
An ordered subset of tools from a schema, used to expose only relevant tools to a consumer.
A pre-authored conversational template attached to a schema; complements tool-based interaction.
A Claude Code workflow definition. Bot-relevant: flowmcp-create-agent is the operational counterpart.
A read-only, addressable artifact exposed via the schema.
Framework for adapting REST APIs into a standardized MCP interface — testable, semantically consistent.
Normative content (spec, CHANGELOG) following RFC2119 keywords. Lives in spec/v{X.Y.Z}/.
Non-normative tonality content (personas, examples, skills, bot bundle). Lives in personas/, examples/, skills/.
Memo 049 principle: spec-derived content is produced by generators in the spec-repo, consumed read-only by downstream tools.
Autogenerated LLM-consumable bundle of the spec. Authoritative for citations.
A single tool endpoint in a schema. Pre-v3 terminology; v4 favours 'Tool'.
An invokable action defined in a schema. Tools have parameters and outputs.
A short identifier (e.g. V001) describing a schema validation rule. Defined in spec/v4.0.0/09-validation-rules.md.
A schema's maturity (e.g. draft, candidate, stable, deprecated). Documented in spec/v4.0.0/21-schema-lifecycle.md.
The grading methodology for schemas. Documented in spec/v4.0.0/22-scoring-protocol.md.
Model Context Protocol — Anthropic's standard for AI-tool interaction.
An endpoint exposing tools via MCP. flowmcp-servers is the reference local/remote implementation.
Payment protocol for monetizing MCP servers using ERC20 on-chain payments.
A target user archetype (Mira, Daniel, Sofia, Anders) used to calibrate documentation tone and depth.
A pre-defined prompt shown in the OpenAI GPT Builder UI when a user opens the bot.
